onboarding.step1Body: Track your Bible reading across all 66 books. Check off chapters as you read and watch your progress grow.
onboarding.step2Body: Share reflections on the blog, discuss scriptures in the forum, and follow others on their reading journey.
onboarding.step3Body: Take quizzes on 12 different Bible themes and earn badges as you complete each level.
onboarding.step4Body: Set a daily chapter goal, track your streak, and use the reading heatmap to stay consistent.
onboarding.step5Body: AI Study Companion, Reading Plans, Study Notes, Messages & Groups — everything you need to go deeper in your study for just $3/month.
onboarding.saveProgressDesc: Create a free account to save your progress across all your devices.

bookmarks.title: Bookmarks
bookmarks.error: Could not update bookmark. Please try again.

bookSummaries: Genesis records the creation of the universe, the fall of humanity, the flood, and the calling of Abraham — establishing the foundation of God's redemptive plan.
Exodus tells the dramatic story of Israel's deliverance from Egypt through Moses, the giving of the Ten Commandments, and the construction of the Tabernacle.
Leviticus provides detailed instructions for Israel's worship, sacrifices, and priestly duties — centered on the call to be holy because God is holy.
Numbers chronicles Israel's 40-year wilderness journey, their repeated failures, and God's faithful provision and discipline as He guides them toward Canaan.
Deuteronomy is Moses' farewell address to Israel before entering Canaan, recalling their history and renewing the covenant with a call to love and obey God.
Joshua records Israel's conquest and settlement of Canaan under Joshua's leadership, showing God's faithfulness in fulfilling His promises to Abraham.
Judges documents a recurring pattern in Israel: they abandon God, face oppression, cry out, and God raises up a deliverer. It reveals humanity's need for a righteous king.
Ruth is a beautiful story of loyal love set during the time of the judges. Ruth's faithfulness to Naomi becomes a picture of God's redeeming grace.
First Samuel covers the transition from judges to kings: the ministry of Samuel, the failed reign of Saul, and the rise of David as God's chosen king.
Second Samuel recounts David's reign in full — his triumphs, his sin with Bathsheba, and the consequences that follow — while showing God's covenant faithfulness.
First Kings covers Solomon's glorious reign, the building of the Temple, and the tragic division of the kingdom, setting the stage for Israel's long decline.
Second Kings records the progressive decline and fall of both Israel (to Assyria) and Judah (to Babylon), showing the consequences of persistent unfaithfulness.
First Chronicles retells Israel's history from a priestly perspective, focusing on David's preparations for the Temple and his efforts to establish proper worship.
Second Chronicles highlights the reigns of the kings of Judah, emphasizing times of revival and reform, and concludes with the decree of Cyrus allowing return from exile.
Ezra records the return of Jewish exiles to Jerusalem under Zerubbabel and later Ezra himself, the rebuilding of the Temple, and spiritual reforms.
Nehemiah tells the story of rebuilding Jerusalem's walls in 52 days despite fierce opposition, followed by spiritual renewal through public reading of the Law.
Esther shows God's hidden providence as Queen Esther risked her life to save the Jewish people from Haman's plot — though God's name is never mentioned.
Job grapples with undeserved suffering through poetic dialogues, ultimately revealing that God's wisdom transcends human understanding and that He is sovereign in all things.
The Psalms are 150 songs and prayers covering the full range of human experience — praise, lament, thanksgiving, and trust — guiding God's people in heartfelt worship.
Proverbs is a collection of wise sayings for living well, emphasizing that true wisdom begins with fearing God and shapes every area of daily life.
Ecclesiastes explores the emptiness of life 'under the sun' apart from God, concluding that fearing God and keeping His commandments is the whole purpose of humanity.
The Song of Solomon celebrates the beauty of romantic love between a bride and groom, often read as a metaphor for the love between God and His people.
Isaiah contains some of the most majestic prophecies in Scripture — judgment on Judah and the nations, comfort for exiles, and vivid descriptions of the Suffering Servant who brings salvation.
Called the 'weeping prophet,' Jeremiah preached God's judgment to a stubborn Judah, while also announcing the glorious promise of a new covenant written on the heart.
Lamentations is a collection of five poetic laments over the destruction of Jerusalem, expressing profound grief while holding on to the truth of God's faithfulness.
Ezekiel, a priest-prophet among the exiles in Babylon, received spectacular visions of God's glory, pronounced judgment on Jerusalem, and proclaimed future restoration.
Daniel blends narrative and apocalyptic visions to show that God is sovereign over empires and history, encouraging faithfulness under pressure through remarkable stories and prophecies.
God commanded Hosea to marry an unfaithful wife as a living parable of Israel's spiritual adultery — and God's relentless love that pursues and restores.
Joel uses a devastating locust plague as a call to repentance, warning of the coming Day of the Lord and promising the outpouring of the Holy Spirit on all people.
Amos, a shepherd turned prophet, declared God's judgment on Israel's social injustice, empty religion, and oppression of the poor, calling for justice to 'roll on like a river.'
The shortest book of the Old Testament pronounces judgment on Edom for rejoicing over Jerusalem's fall, affirming that pride comes before destruction.
Jonah's story of fleeing God's call, being swallowed by a great fish, and ultimately going to Nineveh reveals God's compassion extending beyond Israel to all nations.
Micah alternates between judgment and hope, condemning corruption and injustice while prophesying the Messiah's birth in Bethlehem and calling for justice, mercy, and humility.
Nahum declares the fall of Nineveh — the mighty Assyrian capital — as a demonstration that God is slow to anger but will not leave the guilty unpunished.
Habakkuk wrestles honestly with God about evil and injustice, and receives the answer that 'the righteous shall live by faith' — even when circumstances make no sense.
Zephaniah warns of the coming Day of the Lord's judgment on Judah and the nations, but concludes with a joyful promise that God will rejoice over His restored people with singing.
Haggai delivered four short messages urging the returned exiles to stop neglecting the Temple and rebuild it, promising that God's glory in the new Temple would surpass the old.
Zechariah encouraged the rebuilding of the Temple through eight night visions and rich Messianic prophecies about a humble King entering Jerusalem on a donkey.
Malachi, the last voice of the Old Testament, rebukes priests and people for spiritual apathy and covenant unfaithfulness, closing with a promise of the coming messenger.
Matthew presents Jesus as the fulfillment of Old Testament prophecy and the long-awaited King of Israel, structured around five major discourses including the Sermon on the Mount.
The shortest gospel moves at a rapid pace, emphasizing Jesus' powerful actions and miracles. Mark presents Jesus as the Suffering Servant who came to serve and give His life.
Luke's careful, orderly account emphasizes Jesus' compassion for the poor, outcasts, women, and Gentiles — showing that salvation in Christ is available to all people.
John's deeply theological gospel uses seven signs and seven 'I AM' statements to reveal Jesus as the eternal Son of God, written so readers would believe and have life in His name.
Acts traces the spread of the gospel from Jerusalem to Rome through the power of the Holy Spirit, following the apostles Peter and Paul as the church expands to all nations.
Romans is Paul's most systematic presentation of the gospel — the condemnation of all humanity in sin, the righteousness of God through faith in Christ, and new life in the Spirit.
Paul addresses serious problems in the Corinthian church — divisions, immorality, lawsuits, spiritual gifts, and doubts about resurrection — calling them to unity and love.
Paul's most personal letter defends his apostleship and ministry amid intense suffering, teaching that God's power is made perfect in weakness.
Paul passionately defends the gospel of grace against those adding circumcision as a requirement for salvation, declaring that we are justified by faith alone, not law-keeping.
Ephesians soars with rich teaching on the believer's identity in Christ, the unity of the church as Christ's body, and the call to walk in love while standing firm in spiritual warfare.
Written from prison, Philippians radiates joy in every chapter. Paul urges the church to have the same humble mind as Christ and to find contentment in all circumstances.
Colossians counters early heresy by exalting Christ as the image of God and the head of creation and the church — complete in Him, believers need nothing else.
Paul's earliest letter encourages young believers facing persecution, instructs them on holy living, and gives hope about the resurrection and Christ's return.
Paul corrects misunderstandings about the Day of the Lord (which had not yet come), encouraging endurance under persecution and faithful, diligent living.
Paul writes his young protégé Timothy with instructions for church order, leadership qualifications, and sound doctrine against false teaching in Ephesus.
Paul's final letter, written near death, urges Timothy to remain faithful, preach the Word boldly, and endure suffering — passing on the faith to the next generation.
Paul instructs Titus in organizing the churches of Crete by appointing qualified leaders and teaching sound doctrine that produces godly living.
This brief personal letter appeals to Philemon to receive back his runaway slave Onesimus — now a fellow believer — as a brother in Christ, not as a slave.
Hebrews presents Jesus as superior to angels, Moses, and the Levitical priesthood. It calls Jewish Christians not to drift from faith, supported by a magnificent 'hall of faith.'
James is a practical wisdom letter insisting that genuine saving faith will naturally produce good works, taming the tongue, caring for the poor, and persevering through trials.
Peter writes to scattered, persecuted believers, reminding them of their living hope through Christ's resurrection and calling them to holy conduct among the Gentiles.
Peter warns against false teachers who distort the truth, urges spiritual growth in knowledge of Christ, and assures believers that the Lord will return as promised.
John writes to combat early Gnosticism by affirming the physical reality of Jesus and testing true faith by obedience, love for one another, and confession that Jesus is Lord.
The shortest book in the New Testament warns a local church not to offer hospitality to false teachers who deny the Incarnation, while urging continued love and obedience.
John commends Gaius for welcoming traveling missionaries, rebukes the domineering Diotrephes who refuses them, and upholds Demetrius as a model of faithfulness.
Jude urgently calls believers to defend the faith against false teachers who have secretly infiltrated the church, promising judgment on the ungodly and mercy for the faithful.
Revelation is a prophetic vision of Christ's ultimate victory over evil, the judgment of the nations, and the creation of a new heaven and new earth where God dwells with His people forever.

about.heroSub: Track your reading, connect with fellow students, and grow in your love for God's Word.
about.purposeBody1: JW Study was built with one simple goal: give Bible students a persistent, cross-device tracker for their Bible reading. Whether you're on your phone at the Kingdom Hall, on your laptop at home, or on a tablet during family worship — your progress is always with you.
about.purposeBody2: Beyond tracking, we wanted to create a space where people studying with Jehovah's Witnesses — and active Witnesses themselves — can encourage one another, share thoughts, and grow together as a community of Bible students.
about.card1Body: Those who are studying with Jehovah's Witnesses and want to follow along in the New World Translation as they learn.
about.card2Body: Jehovah's Witnesses looking to log their personal Bible reading and connect with a community that shares the same values.
about.card3Body: Families doing family worship, or study groups wanting to encourage each other's reading progress and share insights.
about.jwBody: JW Study is designed as a companion to the JW Library app. Do your reading in JW Library — where you can access a free digital copy of the New World Translation — then come here to log your chapters, take notes, and share your journey with the community.
about.feat1: Track every chapter — mark chapters read across all 66 books of the Bible, with your progress saved to the cloud so it follows you everywhere.
about.feat2: Save personal notes — write private notes tied to any book and chapter as you study.
about.feat3: Join the community forum — discuss Bible topics, ask questions, and encourage fellow students.
about.feat4: Test your knowledge — take Bible quizzes and earn badges as you grow in your understanding of Scripture.
about.feat5: Follow other readers — see activity from the people you follow and share your own updates with your community.
about.feat6: Share your progress — generate a shareable image of your reading progress to encourage others.
about.creatorBio: Lexx is a Bible student and Jehovah's Witness who built JW Study out of a personal need — a simple, reliable way to track Bible reading across any device, without losing progress. What started as a personal tool grew into a full community platform for others walking the same spiritual journey.
about.ctaSub: Create a free account and begin tracking your Bible reading today.
about.footer: JW Study is an independent community project and is not affiliated with or endorsed by Jehovah's Witnesses or the Watch Tower Society.

history.title: Reading History
history.emptySub: Start marking chapters as read on the tracker and your activity will appear here.

verses.text: For God loved the world so much that he gave his only-begotten Son, so that everyone exercising faith in him might not be destroyed but have everlasting life.
For I well know the thoughts that I am thinking toward you,' declares Jehovah, 'thoughts of peace, and not of calamity, to give you a future and a hope.
For all things I have the strength through the one who gives me power.
We know that God makes all his works cooperate together for the good of those who love God, those who are the ones called according to his purpose.
Trust in Jehovah with all your heart, and do not rely on your own understanding. In all your ways take notice of him, and he will make your paths straight.
But those hoping in Jehovah will regain power. They will soar up with wings like eagles. They will run and not grow weary; they will walk and not tire out.
Jehovah is my Shepherd. I will lack nothing.
Keep on, then, seeking first the Kingdom and his righteousness, and all these other things will be added to you.

By this undeserved kindness you have been saved through faith, and this is not of your own doing; rather, it is God's gift. No, it is not a result of works, so that no one should have grounds for boasting.

All Scripture is inspired of God and beneficial for teaching, for reproving, for setting things straight, for disciplining in righteousness, so that the man of God may be fully competent, completely equipped for every good work.
But he was pierced for our transgression; he was crushed for our errors. He bore the punishment for our peace, and because of his wounds we were healed.
It is because of Jehovah's loyal love that we have not come to our finish, for his mercies never end. They are new each morning; great is your faithfulness!
Have I not commanded you? Be courageous and strong. Do not be struck with terror or filled with dread, for Jehovah your God is with you wherever you go.
God is our refuge and strength, a help that is readily found in times of distress.

For I am convinced that neither death nor life nor angels nor governments nor things now here nor things to come nor powers nor height nor depth nor any other creation will be able to separate us from God's love that is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
Come to me, all you who are toiling and loaded down, and I will refresh you.
Now, however, these three remain: faith, hope, love, the greatest of these being love.
Faith is the assured expectation of what is hoped for, the evident demonstration of realities that are not seen.
He has told you, O man, what is good. And what is Jehovah requiring of you? Only to exercise justice, to cherish loyalty, and to walk in modesty with your God!
Your word is a lamp to my foot, and a light for my path.
Jesus said to him: 'I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.'
Therefore, if anyone is in union with Christ, he is a new creation; the old things passed away; look! new things have come into existence.
And stop being molded by this system of things, but be transformed by making your mind over, so that you may prove to yourselves the good and acceptable and perfect will of God.
Listen, O Israel: Jehovah our God is one Jehovah. You must love Jehovah your God with all your heart and all your soul and all your strength.
The thief comes only to steal and slaughter and destroy. I have come that they may have life and have it in abundance.
I praise you because in an awe-inspiring way I am wonderfully made. Your works are wonderful; I know this very well.

Do not be anxious over anything, but in everything by prayer and supplication along with thanksgiving, let your petitions be made known to God; and the peace of God that surpasses all understanding will guard your hearts and your mental powers by means of Christ Jesus.
In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
But the righteous one will live by his faithfulness.
I will give you a new heart and put a new spirit within you, and I will remove the heart of stone from your body and give you a heart of flesh.
Jehovah your God is in your midst, a mighty one who will save. He will rejoice over you with great joy. He will become silent in his love. He will exult over you with a joyful shout.
Whoever does not love has not come to know God, because God is love.
However, become doers of the word and not hearers only, deceiving yourselves with false reasoning.
Then if my people who are called by my name humble themselves and pray and seek my face and turn away from their evil ways, I will hear from the heavens and forgive their sin and heal their land.
She is clothed with strength and splendor, and she looks to the future with a smile.
Whatever you are doing, whether you speak or act, do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus, thanking God the Father through him.
But you will receive power when the holy spirit comes upon you, and you will be witnesses of me in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the most distant part of the earth.
Where you go I will go, and where you spend the night I will spend the night. Your people will be my people and your God my God.
And who knows whether it is for a time like this that you have attained to your royal status?
For I know that my Redeemer is alive, and that in the end he will stand up over the dust.

If it must be, our God whom we serve is able to rescue us from the burning fiery furnace, O king, and he will rescue us from your hand. But even if he does not, let it be known to you, O king, that we will not serve your gods.
while you throw all your anxiety on him, because he cares for you.
For the joy of Jehovah is your stronghold.
Find exquisite delight in Jehovah, and he will grant you the desires of your heart.
Do not be afraid, for I am with you. Do not be anxious, for I am your God. I will fortify you, yes, I will help you, I will really hold on to you with my right hand of righteousness.
You are the light of the world. A city cannot be hid when located on a mountain.
I am the vine; you are the branches. Whoever remains in union with me and I in union with him, this one bears much fruit; for apart from me you can do nothing at all.
And he will wipe out every tear from their eyes, and death will be no more, neither will mourning nor outcry nor pain be anymore. The former things have passed away.
But God recommends his own love to us in that, while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.
Taste and see that Jehovah is good; happy is the man who takes refuge in him.
But let justice flow down like waters, and righteousness like an ever-flowing stream.
Jehovah is not slow concerning his promise, as some people consider slowness, but he is patient with you because he does not desire anyone to be destroyed but desires all to attain to repentance.
For even the Son of man came, not to be ministered to, but to minister and to give his life as a ransom in exchange for many.
For the Son of man came to seek and to save what was lost.

For the word of God is alive and exerts power and is sharper than any two-edged sword and pierces even to the dividing of soul and spirit, and of joints from the marrow, and is able to discern thoughts and intentions of the heart.
Always be rejoicing. Pray constantly. Give thanks for everything. This is the will of God in union with Christ Jesus for you.
After that I will pour out my spirit on every sort of flesh, and your sons and your daughters will certainly prophesy, your old men will dream dreams, and your young men will see visions.
'Not by a military force, nor by power, but by my spirit,' says Jehovah of armies.

Go, therefore, and make disciples of people of all the nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the holy spirit, teaching them to observe all the things I have commanded you.
As far off as the sunrise is from the sunset, so far off from us he has put our transgressions.
For a child has been born to us, a son has been given to us; and the rulership will rest on his shoulder. His name will be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Eternal Father, Prince of Peace.
I found it necessary to write you to urge you to put up a hard fight for the faith that was once for all time delivered to the holy ones.

Bring the entire tithe into the storehouse, so that there may be food in my house; and test me out, please, in this regard,' says Jehovah of armies, 'to see whether I will not open to you the floodgates of the heavens and pour out on you a blessing until there is nothing lacking.

Happy is the man who does not walk according to the advice of the wicked and does not stand on the path of sinners and does not sit in the seat of scoffers. But his delight is in the law of Jehovah, and he reads His law in an undertone day and night.

But he said to me: 'My undeserved kindness is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.' Most gladly, then, I will boast about my weaknesses, in order that the power of the Christ may remain over me like a tent.
Jehovah will fight for you, and you will keep silent.
